signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice OFFER IUI (intrauterine insemination)? Answer yes when 'IUI', 'intrauterine insemination', 'artificial insemination', or 'in-office insemination' appears in a service context — a services page, service-menu item, procedures list, a fertility service page, or a physician/provider bio or specialty. A service-menu item or a fertility service page that lists IUI counts. Do NOT infer from generic words like 'fertility', 'consultation', 'treatment', or 'family planning' without one of these insemination terms, and do NOT answer yes when the term appears ONLY in a blog or health-education article explaining what IUI is, with no sign the practice performs it (that is not_found). Evidence: quote the surrounding text and cite the specific page URL it came from (e.g. /services/fertility), not just the homepage.
signals.note: Core fit, heaviest signal. The client called IUI out specifically for Michigan: a listed in-office IUI service line is the clearest match for the office-based fertility work FemaSeed upgrades. Kept DISTINCT from the broad fertility-services signal, not folded in. Phrase-bound and service-context-bound so it does not fire on generic fertility copy or editorial content.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice OFFER fertility or infertility services? Answer yes when the website shows the practice provides fertility / infertility care as a service. Qualifying service-context evidence is ANY of: (a) a named service or service-menu item such as 'Fertility', 'Infertility', 'Fertility Care', 'Family Building', or 'PCOS'; (b) a dedicated fertility/infertility service page (e.g. a /fertility or /services/fertility URL, or a page whose heading is Fertility/Infertility); (c) a statement that the practice treats, manages, evaluates, or offers it — e.g. 'we treat infertility', 'fertility services', 'infertility evaluation', 'infertility workup', 'fertility consultation', 'fertility treatment', 'fertility testing', 'ovulation induction', 'PCOS treatment', 'recurrent pregnancy loss', 'irregular periods', or 'hormone imbalance'; or (d) a provider bio listing fertility or infertility as a specialty or clinical interest. The signal is whether the practice OFFERS fertility care — a bare service-menu item like 'Fertility' or 'PCOS', or a fertility service page, DOES count. Do NOT answer yes when fertility/infertility appears ONLY in a blog post, news/press item, patient-education article, or testimonial with no sign the practice offers it (e.g. an educational article 'understanding infertility' with no fertility service listed) — that is not_found. When the term appears among the practice's listed services or on a service page, answer yes. Evidence: quote the menu item or sentence and cite the specific page URL (e.g. /services/fertility).
signals.note: Broad fertility-activity fit. Collapses the v9 medicated-cycle / infertility-eval / fertility-consult signals. v10 relaxed the binding after a confirmed false negative (a practice with a 'Fertility' service-menu item, a /service/fertility page, and 'treats infertility' on its about page was scored not_found): service-context evidence — a named service, a dedicated service page, a 'we treat/offer' statement, or a provider specialty — now counts, not only verbatim multi-word phrases. The editorial guard still keeps blog / patient-education mentions out.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice perform fertility cycle monitoring by ultrasound? Answer yes ONLY when one of these phrases appears verbatim: 'follicle monitoring', 'follicular monitoring', 'cycle monitoring', 'monitoring ultrasound', 'transvaginal ultrasound', 'saline sonohysterogram', 'sonohysterogram', 'hysterosonography', 'SIS/FemVue', or 'saline infusion sonogram'. Do NOT match the bare word 'ultrasound' or 'monitoring' alone. 'Obstetric ultrasound' or 'pregnancy ultrasound' alone = not_found — the phrase must be tied to gynecologic or cycle-related (fertility) context. SERVICE-CONTEXT RULE: must appear as a listed service or procedure, not in a blog or education article. Evidence: quote the surrounding sentence and cite the specific page URL.
signals.note: A plus, not a gate. Cycle-monitoring ultrasound (incl. saline sonohysterogram / SIS) reinforces that fertility treatment happens in-office and adds confidence, but its absence is not disqualifying, so it carries a modest fit weight and no penalty when not found.

signals.signal_label: FemVue or FemaSeed mentioned (existing Femasys relationship)
signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice mention FemVue or FemaSeed anywhere on the site? Answer yes when any of these exact Femasys product names appears verbatim: 'FemVue', 'SIS/FemVue', 'FemVue saline', or 'FemaSeed'. A FemaSeed mention is the strongest possible signal — the practice already uses Femasys's own FemaSeed product (the product being sold), not just a related one. Evidence: quote the surrounding sentence and cite the page URL it came from, and make clear in the quote whether it is FemVue or FemaSeed.
signals.note: Single strongest warm-lead signal — an existing Femasys relationship. Matches FemVue (the tubal-assessment product) OR FemaSeed (the product being sold); either on a practice's own site means they already use Femasys. A FemaSeed mention is the strongest of all: the practice is already a FemaSeed user, so the motion is expansion / reference, NOT a cold sell (operator should decide suppress-vs-expand). Its power is the floor_tier (a confirmed practice never falls below Contender), NOT a heavy fit weight: a very-high fit weight would inflate max_positive and cap every non-relationship practice below Bullseye, contradicting the rule that this signal is not required for Bullseye. Weight is therefore bounded; floor_tier carries the strength.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website show explicit cash-pay / self-pay capability or third-party patient financing? Answer yes when any of these appears verbatim in a service, billing, or patient-information context: branded financing — 'CareCredit', 'Care Credit', 'Ally Lending', 'Alphaeon', 'Cherry financing', 'Prosper Healthcare', 'PatientFi', 'GreenSky'; account types — 'FSA accepted', 'FSA eligible', 'HSA accepted', 'HSA eligible', 'flexible spending', 'health savings account'; or plain-English cash-pay — 'cash pay', 'cash-pay', 'cash accepted', 'cash patients welcome', 'cash patients', 'self-pay', 'self pay', 'self-pay patients', 'no insurance required', 'insurance not required', 'patients without insurance', 'uninsured patients', 'financing available', 'payment plans available', 'payment plan', 'interest-free financing', '0% financing', 'monthly payment', 'flexible payment', 'affordable payment', 'we offer financing', or 'financing options'. GUARD: 'We accept most insurance plans' alone = not_found (insurance context, not cash-pay). 'Call for pricing' alone = not_found (ambiguous). Must be an explicit statement of cash or financing availability. Evidence: quote the surrounding sentence and cite the page URL.
signals.note: READINESS — confidence only, never qualifies alone. Direct-billing / financing infrastructure predicts adoption velocity for a patient-pay device workflow. positive_weight is 0 so it contributes to confidence_score (a confirmed yes raises the confidence blend) but NEVER to fit_signal_score — a cash-pay-only practice has fit 0 and stays below the 50-point Manual Review floor. v10 fixes the v9-MI bug where this signal claimed 'confidence only' but shipped weight 16, letting cash-pay inflate fit. v10 also adds the plain-English phrases the prior version missed entirely.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice perform in-office contraceptive DEVICE procedures (insertion/removal)? Answer yes ONLY when one of these appears verbatim as an offered procedure: 'IUD insertion', 'IUD placement', 'IUD removal', 'Mirena insertion', 'Kyleena insertion', 'Liletta insertion', 'Paragard insertion', 'Nexplanon insertion', 'Nexplanon removal', 'contraceptive implant insertion', 'birth control implant', 'long-acting reversible contraception', or 'LARC'. Do NOT match the bare words 'IUD', 'birth control', or 'contraception' on their own — the full procedure phrase must be present. GUARD: 'We prescribe birth control' = not_found (oral / no device procedure). 'IUD' in a blog explaining contraceptive options = not_found. Must appear as a listed procedure or service offering. SERVICE-CONTEXT RULE: services page, procedures list, or treatment menu only. Evidence: quote the surrounding sentence and cite the page URL.
signals.note: Procedural-maturity fit (readiness-adjacent), medium weight. Confirms an in-office device insertion/removal workflow — the same procedural infrastructure FemaSeed adoption requires. Scores as warm fit, not strong fit, and is deliberately too light to qualify a practice on its own (contraceptive-only stays below the Manual Review floor). Not a fertility signal per se.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice operate as, or hold itself out as, an IVF or reproductive-endocrinology fertility center? Answer yes when any of these appears as a listed SERVICE or as the practice identity: 'IVF', 'in vitro fertilization', 'egg retrieval', 'embryo transfer', 'reproductive endocrinology', 'reproductive endocrinologist', 'REI', 'egg freezing', 'sperm freezing', 'embryo freezing', 'cryopreservation', 'egg donation', 'donor eggs', 'gestational carrier', 'surrogacy', or the practice naming itself a 'fertility center' (name, tagline, or specialty description). REFER-VS-OFFER GUARD: 'we refer patients for IVF when needed' in a community OBGYN context is NOT an exclusion — the practice is not offering IVF. Distinguish 'we offer IVF' (exclude) from 'we refer for IVF' (do not exclude). The exclusion fires only when IVF/REI is a listed in-house service or the practice's own identity is REI. Evidence: quote the surrounding sentence and cite the page URL.
signals.note: Exclusion. A confirmed yes means a specialized fertility center that routes patients to in-house ART and male-factor pathways, not the office-based OBGYN profile FemaSeed upgrades. The NPI taxonomy structural gate (rei_on_staff) pre-filters most before crawl; this signal catches any that reach the LLM pass. The refer-vs-offer guard prevents excluding community OBGYNs who merely refer out for IVF. Hospital / health-system / academic practices are removed by the structural exclusion rules in run_config, not by a textual signal.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice offer elective, aesthetic, or cosmetic services that are typically patient-pay? Answer yes when one or more of these is explicitly named as an offered service: medical aesthetics — 'Botox', 'Dysport', 'Xeomin', 'dermal filler', 'lip filler', 'Juvederm', 'Restylane', 'Sculptra', 'Kybella', 'CoolSculpting', 'body contouring', 'laser hair removal', 'IPL', 'microneedling', 'chemical peel', 'HydraFacial'; intimate/aesthetic gyn — 'Mona Lisa Touch', 'vaginal rejuvenation', 'labiaplasty', 'O-Shot', 'PRP therapy', 'ovarian PRP'; weight management — 'semaglutide', 'tirzepatide', 'GLP-1', 'medical weight loss', 'weight loss program', 'weight management program'; spa/wellness — 'med spa', 'medspa', 'medical spa', 'IV therapy', 'IV infusion', 'vitamin infusion', 'wellness program'; in-office elective devices — 'Emsella', 'EmSculpt', 'ThermiVa', 'FemiLift', 'Votiva', 'NovaSure', 'Cerene'. GUARD: a blog post about 'what is CoolSculpting' = not_found; a patient testimonial mentioning Botox = not_found. Must appear on a services page, procedures list, or treatment menu. Evidence: quote the surrounding sentence and cite the page URL.
signals.note: READINESS — confidence only, never qualifies alone. A practice running a cash-pay elective service line already has the billing workflow for non-insurance charges, a strong operational readiness signal for a patient-pay device. positive_weight is 0: like S-MI-005 it raises confidence when confirmed but contributes nothing to fit, so elective services can never qualify a practice on their own.
